questions.question: What is the main advantage of CSS Custom Properties over Sass variables?
options: CSS variables can be changed at runtime with JavaScript and participate in the cascade
CSS variables are supported in IE11
questions.answer: CSS variables can be changed at runtime with JavaScript and participate in the cascade
questions.explanation: Unlike Sass variables which are compiled away at build time, CSS custom properties are live in the browser and can be updated dynamically via JavaScript or media queries.

questions.question: What syntax is used to access a CSS variable named --color-primary?
questions.explanation: CSS custom properties are accessed using the var() function: var(--property-name, optional-fallback).

questions.question: Where should global CSS variables be defined?
questions.explanation: :root represents the highest-level element (the html element) and has higher specificity, making variables defined there available globally throughout the document.

questions.question: How do you implement dark mode using CSS variables?
options: Duplicate all CSS with different colour values
Use JavaScript to change all inline styles
Redefine variable values within a [data-theme='dark'] selector or prefers-color-scheme media query
Use the inverted CSS filter on the body
questions.answer: Redefine variable values within a [data-theme='dark'] selector or prefers-color-scheme media query
questions.explanation: By redefining :root variables in a dark theme selector, all components that use those variables automatically update without any additional CSS changes.

questions.question: What is the fallback syntax for var() when a variable might not be set?
questions.explanation: The second argument to var() is the fallback value, used when the custom property is not defined: var(--color-accent, #f43f5e).
